events.title: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus with Hyperglycemia Diagnosed

<Conclusion;Findings>: Left Ventricle: Normal cavity size.
Concentric left ventricular hypertrophy with interventricular septum and posterior wall measuring 13 mm and 12 mm respectively.
Preserved systolic function with ejection fraction estimated at 58% by biplane Simpson method.
No regional wall motion abnormalities.
Grade I diastolic dysfunction (impaired relaxation pattern).
Left Atrium: Mildly dilated at 42 mm.
Left atrial volume index at upper limit of normal.
Right Heart: Normal right ventricular size and systolic function.
TAPSE 22 mm.
Estimated RV systolic pressure 32 mmHg.
Normal right atrial size.
No RV hypertrophy.
Valves: Aortic valve is trileaflet with mild sclerosis but no stenosis.
Trace aortic regurgitation.
Mitral valve shows mild annular calcification.
Trace to mild mitral regurgitation.
Tricuspid valve normal with trace regurgitation used for RV pressure estimation.
Pulmonic valve normal.
Other: Normal aortic root diameter.
No pericardial effusion.
IVC normal caliber with appropriate respiratory variation.
Impression: Concentric left ventricular hypertrophy consistent with longstanding hypertension.
Mild aortic valve sclerosis without stenosis.
Normal right heart function and pressures.
<Clinical Correlation;Clinical Indicatio: Findings consistent with hypertensive heart disease.
Recommend optimal blood pressure control and follow-up echocardiography in 2 years or sooner if symptoms change.
Type 2 diabetes mellitus with recent lab findings of elevated creatinine (1.1 mg/dL), reduced eGFR (62 mL/min/1.73m²), and microalbuminuria (ACR 47.4 mg/g).
Evaluate for diabetic nephropathy and renal structural changes.
Technique: Real-time B-mode and Doppler ultrasound evaluation of both kidneys and urinary bladder performed in supine and lateral decubitus positions.
Comparison: No prior renal ultrasound available for comparison.
<Conclusion;Findings>: Right Kidney: Measures 10.2 x 4.8 x 4.5 cm.
Normal position and orientation.
The renal parenchyma shows mildly increased echogenicity (Grade I) compared to the adjacent liver parenchyma, suggestive of early parenchymal disease.
Cortical thickness measures approximately 1.3 cm (lower limit of normal).
No focal lesions identified.
No hydronephrosis or calculi.
Normal corticomedullary differentiation is partially maintained.
Left Kidney: Measures 10.0 x 4.6 x 4.3 cm.
Similar findings to the right kidney with mildly increased parenchymal echogenicity (Grade I).
Cortical thickness approximately 1.2 cm.
A simple cortical cyst measuring 1.2 x 1.0 cm is noted at the upper pole, meeting all criteria for a Bosniak category I cyst.
No hydronephrosis, calculi, or solid masses.
Renal Doppler: Bilateral renal arteries demonstrate normal waveform morphology.
Resistive index (RI) measures 0.72 on the right and 0.74 on the left (upper limit of normal 0.70).
No evidence of renal artery stenosis.
Mildly elevated RI bilaterally may reflect early intrarenal vascular changes consistent with the clinical history of hypertension and diabetes.
Urinary Bladder: Adequately distended.
Normal wall thickness.
No intraluminal lesions or calculi.
Post-void residual volume: 25 mL (normal).
Impression: Bilateral mildly increased renal parenchymal echogenicity (Grade I), consistent with early medical renal disease.
In the clinical context of diabetes and hypertension with documented microalbuminuria and reduced eGFR, findings are suggestive of early diabetic nephropathy.
Mildly elevated resistive indices bilaterally (0.72–0.74), which may reflect early renovascular changes secondary to hypertension and/or diabetes.
Simple left upper pole renal cyst (Bosniak I) — no follow-up required.
No hydronephrosis, calculi, or obstructive uropathy.
Recommendation: Consider nephrology referral for comprehensive diabetic kidney disease management.
Follow-up renal ultrasound in 12 months if clinically indicated.

<Clinical Dx.;Medical History and Clinic: Essential hypertension, Type 2 diabetes mellitus, Mild persistent asthma
<Conclusion;Findings>: CHEST X-RAY PA AND LATERAL
The heart is mildly enlarged with cardiothoracic ratio of 0.54 (previously 0.52).
Left ventricular configuration.
The aorta is mildly unfolded, consistent with age and hypertension history.
Lungs show mild bilateral peribronchial thickening, more pronounced in the lower lobes, consistent with the known history of asthma.
No focal consolidation, mass, or pleural effusion.
No pneumothorax.
The mediastinum is midline.
No mediastinal widening or lymphadenopathy.
The trachea is midline.
The bony thorax is unremarkable.
The visualized upper abdomen is unremarkable.
<Clinical Correlation;Clinical Indicatio: Evaluate cardiac silhouette and lung fields.
Impression: Mild cardiomegaly with left ventricular configuration, slightly progressed compared to prior study.
Bilateral peribronchial thickening consistent with known asthma,
